title: Ligand-confined zinc slurries enable durable flow-based energy storage - Nature Energy
text:
Zinc-based batteries could offer long-duration energy storage, yet conventional zinc electrodes degrade during cycling. Now, a flowing zinc slurry battery is developed with zinc nanoparticles dispersed in a conductive network and a ligand that stabilizes the slurry. The slurry shows reversible Zn/Zn2+ conversion and enables durable battery operation.

ReferencesWang, F. et al. Highly reversible zinc metal anode for aqueous batteries. Nat. Mater. 17, 543–549 (2018). This study reports that electrolyte-regulated Zn2+ solvation can enable efficient Zn plating/stripping and long-lived aqueous Zn full cells.Article 

Google Scholar 
Ma, L. et al. Realizing high zinc reversibility in rechargeable batteries. Nat. Energy 5, 743–749 (2020). This perspective argues that claims of highly reversible Zn plating/stripping must be evaluated against standardized, application-relevant protocols, helping to define realistic targets for rechargeable Zn metal batteries.Article 

Google Scholar 
Xu, Z., Li, Y., Li, G., Zhang, H. & Wang, X. Reversible zinc powder anode via crystal facet engineering. Matter 6, 3075–3086 (2023). This study shows that crystal-facet engineering can improve the reversibility, corrosion resistance and practical full-cell performance of Zn powder anodes under deeper discharge conditions than typically used in conventional studies.Article 

Google Scholar 
Yang, Z. et al. A semi-solid zinc powder-based slurry anode for advanced aqueous zinc-ion batteries. Angew. Chem. Int. Ed. 135, e202215306 (2023). This paper introduces a semi-solid Zn slurry anode in which a conductive rheological network and zincophilic tin seeds alleviate agglomerative deposition, volume change and corrosion in powder-based Zn electrodes.Article
